contentBlocks.original:
कदम्बवनचारिणीं मुनिकदम्बकादम्बिनीं
नितम्बजितभूधरां सुरनितम्बिनीसेविताम् ।
नवाम्बुरुहलोचनामभिनवाम्बुदश्यामलां
त्रिलोचनकुटुम्बिनीं त्रिपुरसुन्दरीमाश्रये ॥ १ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kadambavanachāriṇīṃ munikadambakādambinīṃ
nitambajitabhūdharāṃ suranitambinīsēvitām ।
navāmburuhalōchanāmabhinavāmbudaśyāmalāṃ
trilōchanakuṭumbinīṃ tripurasundarīmāśrayē ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: She who roams in the kadamba forest, the very cloud refreshing the assembly of sages, whose hips surpass the beauty of mountains, served by divine women — with fresh lotus eyes — I meditate on that Tripura Sundari.

contentBlocks.original:
कदम्बवनवासिनीं कनकवल्लकीधारिणीं
महार्हमणिहारिणीं मुखसमुल्लसद्वारुणीम् ।
दयाविभवकारिणीं विशदरोचनाचारिणीं
त्रिलोचनकुटुम्बिनीं त्रिपुरसुन्दरीमाश्रये ॥ २ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kadambavanavāsinīṃ kanakavallakīdhāriṇīṃ
mahārhamaṇihāriṇīṃ mukhasamullasadvāruṇīm ।
dayāvibhavakāriṇīṃ viśadarōchanāchāriṇīṃ
trilōchanakuṭumbinīṃ tripurasundarīmāśrayē ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: She who dwells in the kadamba forest, bearing a golden vina, wearing a magnificent gem-studded necklace, her face radiant with bliss — compassionate bestower of grace, of clear and spotless luster — I meditate on that Tripura Sundari.

contentBlocks.original:
कदम्बवनशालया कुचभरोल्लसन्मालया
कुचोपमितशैलया गुरुकृपालसद्वेलया ।
मदारुणकपोलया मधुरगीतवाचालया
कयापि घननीलया कवचिता वयं लीलया ॥ ३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kadambavanaśālayā kuchabharōllasanmālayā
kuchōpamitaśailayā gurukṛpālasadvēlayā ।
madāruṇakapōlayā madhuragītavāchālayā
kayāpi ghananīlayā kavachitā vayaṃ līlayā ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: She of the kadamba grove, with garlands radiant upon her bosom, mountains rivaled by her breasts, flowing with the grace of the Guru — with flushed glowing cheeks, of sweet speech and song — I meditate on that Tripura Sundari.

contentBlocks.original:
कदम्बवनमध्यगां कनकमण्डलोपस्थितां
षडम्बुरुहवासिनीं सततसिद्धसौदामिनीम् ।
विडम्बितजपारुचिं विकचचन्द्रचूडामणिं
त्रिलोचनकुटुम्बिनीं त्रिपुरसुन्दरीमाश्रये ॥ ४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kadambavanamadhyagāṃ kanakamaṇḍalōpasthitāṃ
ṣaḍamburuhavāsinīṃ satatasiddhasaudāminīm ।
viḍambitajapāruchiṃ vikachachandrachūḍāmaṇiṃ
trilōchanakuṭumbinīṃ tripurasundarīmāśrayē ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: She who abides at the center of the kadamba forest, seated on a golden throne, dwelling in the six chakra-lotuses, eternally the lightning-flash of the perfected siddhas — of the splendor of the red hibiscus, with blooming lotus eyes — I meditate on that Tripura Sundari.

contentBlocks.original:
कुचाञ्चितविपञ्चिकां कुटिलकुन्तलालङ्कृतां
कुशेशयनिवासिनीं कुटिलचित्तविद्वेषिणीम् ।
मदारुणविलोचनां मनसिजारिसम्मोहिनीं
मतङ्गमुनिकन्यकां मधुरभाषिणीमाश्रये ॥ ५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kuchāñchitavipañchikāṃ kuṭilakuntalālaṅkṛtāṃ
kuśēśayanivāsinīṃ kuṭilachittavidvēṣiṇīm ।
madāruṇavilōchanāṃ manasijārisammōhinīṃ
mataṅgamunikanyakāṃ madhurabhāṣiṇīmāśrayē ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: She whose vina rests adorned upon her bosom, her flowing locks curling and adorned — dwelling in the lotus of the heart, destroyer of wicked and crooked minds — with intoxicated reddened eyes, the beloved consort of Shiva — I meditate on that Tripura Sundari.

contentBlocks.original:
स्मरेत्प्रथमपुष्पिणीं रुधिरबिन्दुनीलाम्बरां
गृहीतमधुपात्रिकां मदविघूर्णनेत्राञ्चलाम् ।
घनस्तनभरोन्नतां गलितचूलिकां श्यामलां
त्रिलोचनकुटुम्बिनीं त्रिपुरसुन्दरीमाश्रये ॥ ६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
smarētprathamapuṣpiṇīṃ rudhirabindunīlāmbarāṃ
gṛhītamadhupātrikāṃ madavighūrṇanētrāñchalām ।
ghanastanabharōnnatāṃ galitachūlikāṃ śyāmalāṃ
trilōchanakuṭumbinīṃ tripurasundarīmāśrayē ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: One should meditate on her as the first-bloomed, wearing garments of deep red, holding a cup filled with wine, her eyes rolling in blissful intoxication — with a bosom heavy and exalted — I meditate on that Tripura Sundari.

contentBlocks.original:
सकुङ्कुमविलेपनामलकचुम्बिकस्तूरिकां
समन्दहसितेक्षणां सशरचापपाशाङ्कुशाम् ।
अशेषजनमोहिनीमरुणमाल्यभूषाम्बरां
जपाकुसुमभासुरां जपविधौ स्मराम्यम्बिकाम् ॥ ७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sakuṅkumavilēpanāmalakachumbikastūrikāṃ
samandahasitēkṣaṇāṃ saśarachāpapāśāṅkuśām ।
aśēṣajanamōhinīmaruṇamālyabhūṣāmbarāṃ
japākusumabhāsurāṃ japavidhau smarāmyambikām ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Smeared with saffron paste, her tresses kissed by bees drawn to the musk fragrance — with a gentle sweet smile playing in her glance, bearing the bow, arrow, noose and goad — the enchantress of all beings, adorned with red garlands — I meditate on that Tripura Sundari.

contentBlocks.original:
पुरन्दरपुरन्ध्रिकाचिकुरबन्धसैरन्ध्रिकां
पितामहपतिव्रतापटुपटीरचर्चारताम् ।
मुकुन्दरमणीमणीलसदलङ्क्रियाकारिणीं
भजामि भुवनाम्बिकां सुरवधूटिकाचेटिकाम् ॥ ८ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who serves as handmaid to Sachi (Indra's consort) for tying her tresses, who was the skilled sandal-paste attendant for Saraswati (Brahma's consort) — the luminous gem-jewel, the beloved of Mukunda (Vishnu), shining with pristine loveliness — I meditate on that Tripura Sundari.

contentBlocks.original: इति श्रीमत्परमहंसपरिव्राजकाचार्यस्य श्रीगोविन्दभगवत्पूज्यपादशिष्यस्य श्रीमच्छङ्करभगवतः कृतौ त्रिपुरसुन्दर्यष्टकम् ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrīmatparamahaṃsaparivrājakāchāryasya śrīgōvindabhagavatpūjyapādaśiṣyasya śrīmachChaṅkarabhagavataḥ kṛtau tripurasundaryaṣṭakam ।
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Tripura Sundari Ashtakam composed by Shankaracharya, disciple of the venerable Govinda Bhagavatpada, most revered among wandering ascetics of the Paramahamsa order. (Colophon)
